mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound, the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, providing a persistent positive tailwind to forward S&P 500 returns regardless of short-term macro noise. This is an always-on valuation-support channel.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during zero-rate periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in UNRATE exceeding 1.5 percentage points signals an acute labor-market shock that depresses consumer income expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as markets price in demand destruction.
failure_condition: Following triggered instances of UNRATE spikes > 1.5pp, SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 55% of cases, invalidating the negative directional claim.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is simultaneously at the zero bound and PAYEMS is contracting sharply, aggressive monetary accommodation is being deployed specifically to offset an acute labor shock; the joint configuration of near-zero rates plus collapsing payrolls produces a policy-put dynamic that supports equities more strongly than either signal alone, yielding positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=139680.0 as of 2020-04 reflecting historic collapse
failure_condition: In periods where both trigger conditions hold jointly, forward 20d SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either single proxy (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime following an unprecedented collapse in retail activity, an early rebound in RSAFS and stabilization in HOUST signals pent-up demand release and consumer re-engagement, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a full quarter as the market re-rates growth expectations upward from depressed lows.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month increase after two consecutive prior declines
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=444491.0 as of 2020-04 reflecting sharp contraction
failure_condition: RSAFS rebound signals fail to precede positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of historical post-recession analogues, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month decline in INDPRO reflects broad industrial capacity destruction that reduces forward corporate earnings capacity, exerting downward pressure on S&P 500 valuations over a quarterly horizon as production shortfalls translate into weaker realized profits.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=105.0728 as of 2020-03 showing decline from prior peak
failure_condition: Negative correlation between INDPRO trend and 60d forward SPX returns is not observed (Rank IC < 0.03) across two consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month collapse in RSAFS exceeding 10% signals an abrupt consumption-side demand shock severe enough to trigger immediate risk-off repricing in equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the very short 5-day horizon as markets react to the release before broader macro data confirms the trend.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=444491.0 as of 2020-04 following record monthly declines
failure_condition: Following RSAFS declines exceeding 10%, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears when tested outside the initial COVID-shock sample.

mechanism_narrative: Short-horizon momentum in weekly-adjacent labor releases, proxied by the monthly PAYEMS change, drives immediate 5-day equity price reaction as traders extrapolate the pace of labor market deterioration or improvement into near-term earnings revisions, with declining PAYEMS producing negative next-week returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=139680.0 as of 2020-04 reflecting historic monthly decline
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5d forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near zero and CPIAUCSL simultaneously shows month-over-month deflation, the joint signal indicates a liquidity trap risk where monetary policy loses traction on aggregate demand; equities react negatively over the next 20 trading days as deflationary expectations raise real debt burdens and compress corporate margins despite nominal rate accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=237.633 as of 2020-03 amid disinflationary pressure
regime_hint: zero-rate liquidity trap with deflation risk
failure_condition: In periods where both trigger conditions hold, SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction term shows no significant incremental predictive power over either series alone (p>0.10).
